editorial.overview: The biggest Bowman parallel ladder in years. Three parent card types — NBA base, paper NCAA prospects, and Chrome NCAA prospects — each with its own full rainbow. Six parallel families do the work: paper Border, Chrome's core refractor ladder, Reptilian pattern variants, Delight-exclusive Geometric, Sapphire-box Sapphires, and Mega-exclusive Mojo. The format matters: Orange /25, Black /10, and Black Pattern /10 live only in Hobby. Mojo only lives in Mega. Sapphire only lives in Sapphire. Chase what matches your box.

perGroupNotes.Base: The NBA 200-card set carries every parallel family. Purple Border /199 is the ladder floor; Platinum Border /1 and Chrome SuperFractor /1 are the twin paper + chrome peaks. Hobby is the only way to get Orange /25 and the two Black /10s.
perGroupNotes.Paper Prospects: NCAA paper prospects run only the Border ladder — no Chrome variants on paper. Same twelve colors as the NBA base Border family, different odds, different card pool.
perGroupNotes.Chrome Prospects: NCAA Chrome prospects run the same five Chrome families as the NBA base (no Border). The SuperFractor /1 is Hobby + Jumbo only — retail buyers cannot pull a Chrome Prospect /1 from Value or Bulk packs.

rainbowSummary.headlineStat: 147 total parallels across three ladders — 77 for the NBA base set, 58 for Chrome Prospects, and 12 paper Border variants on NCAA prospects.

groups.name: Base

parallels.name: Chrome Rose Gold Mini-Diamond Refractor

parallels.name: Chrome SuperFractor
parallels.note: 1-of-1 — the chrome holy grail. Rainbow gradient.

groups.name: Paper Prospects
groups.appliesTo: 100-card NCAA paper prospect set (AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson, Cameron Boozer, JuJu Watkins, etc.)

groups.name: Chrome Prospects
